scope.section_5_4_rule_3: cleanliness scope = only violations newly introduced in this diff

advisory_observations.section: Step 3 — Mascot scoring + Session self-summary + User rating prompt (M3/M4 additions)
advisory_observations.rationale: All M3/M4 additions are written as conditional rules with explicit triggers ('If verdict is X, do Y'); rating <options> XML is a literal contract artifact; Session Summary requirements are bulleted MUST clauses with named coverage axes. NO narrative/philosophy prose.

advisory_observations.section: Forced-override 'When to use / What it preserves / What it skips'
advisory_observations.rationale: Pre-existing relative to this diff's baseline. Not a NEW verbosity violation. Per default-safe rule, file-level findings without explicit positive new-in-this-diff marker cannot force CLOSE: NO.
Procedural contract for shard detection + Case 1/2/3 branches. Each case is a conditional rule with explicit triggers and outputs. Not narrative.
Each branch is a conditional rule with PASS/FAIL/N-A outcomes and explicit codex_status preconditions. Dense but rule-shaped, not narrative.

advisory_observations.section: Phase 2 — BULK=false candidate-set / staging whitelist (M5 BULK guard)
advisory_observations.rationale: BULK=true vs BULK=false vs no-dev-report cases are explicit conditional rules with named outcomes. foreign_session_candidate exclusion + staged-file count guard are MUST clauses with concrete thresholds (len(modified)+len(created)+30). No narrative prose.

Explicit warn-only behavior with named scope (non-bulk only), clear decision deferral to Phase 2. Three lines of explanation distinguish warn-only from staging-decision authority — necessary precision, not narrative.
Mandated by CLAUDE.md user rule #11: 'Subagent prompts must explicitly list what is FORBIDDEN, not just what is allowed'. Removing this would violate global policy.

Pre-existing relative to this diff's baseline. Meta-prose ('SOLE binding landing per user requirement lines 51-53') exists to prevent the rule from being moved/duplicated and is itself rule-defining. Not a NEW verbosity violation.

advisory_observations.section: Structured Final Status Output (commit_status / failure_code enumeration)
advisory_observations.rationale: Tabular enumeration of commit_status values, failure_code values, and JSON output schema. Each row is a concrete contract for downstream /commit retry protocol consumption. Schema, not narrative.
